I really like the game, the mods are very fun and the long arms give great advantage.
But there is one big problem, i got banned for cheating, i was just in a public infection lobby just having fun and trying out the mods for fun, and then i got banned for cheating.

Why ban someone when the game literally has the mods open to the public for anyone to play with.

If you dont want anyone using mods on the game, make a feature were if your in infection mode, the mods will be locked, until you switch to modded lobby, and put a sign saying " please do not use mods in infection and hunt" 
If i had known that, i would not have been using the mods. Once the game changes one day, ill just keep the review to 3 stars.

Project Cheetah is an awesome free game that’s super fun to play. One of the best things about it is that it offers free mods that really spice up the gameplay. The mods are easy to get and add a lot of variety, making every session different and exciting.

The community and staff are really nice and helpful, which makes the whole experience even better. Plus, you get a free stick and other mods included, so you can jump right into the fun without any extra cost.

If you’re looking for a game with cool mods, friendly people, and zero price tag, Project Cheetah is definitely worth checking out!

every single mod works there's modded game mode great staff community great owner kind and cool there's so many words that i can use to describe this game but i could write 17 paragraphs this game is awesome on every single level

Project cheetah 
is a fast-paced, motion-controlled multiplayer game where the core mechanic is simple yet exhilarating: swing your arms, chase your friends, and tag them before they tag you. The game turns the classic playground tag into a high-energy virtual sport, with intuitive controls that mimic real-life movements—ducking, lunging, and of course, wildly swinging your arms.

What Works Well:
	•	Intuitive Motion Controls: The arm-swinging feels natural and responsive, making every movement count.
	•	Fitness Bonus: It’s a surprisingly good cardio workout disguised as a game.
	•	Variety of Arenas: From neon-lit urban rooftops to floating islands, the arenas add a fun twist to each round.
	•	Multiplayer Chaos: The best moments happen when multiple players are frantically trying to avoid the “it” player—it’s chaotic in the best way.

What Could Be Better:
	•	Tracking Sensitivity: In some setups, arm-tracking can misread a swing, which is frustrating in tight matches.
	•	Limited Solo Content: Without friends (or active online lobbies), the game loses much of its charm.
	•	Fatigue Factor: Extended sessions can become tiring, which is both a plus (exercise!) and a minus (session length).

Verdict:project cheetah succeeds in translating a childhood classic into an energetic digital playground. It’s not the most deep or complex game, but its blend of movement, chaos, and friendly competition makes it a blast at parties or for short bursts of active perspective of someone exhausted after playing?
